heroHeadline: SAF Launches Coordinated Multi-State Offensive Across 6 States; RSF Drone Kills 13 in El-Obeid Residential House Including 8 Children
heroSubtitle: The Sudanese Armed Forces launched their most extensive multi-front campaign in months on April 22-24, striking RSF positions across six states and claiming destruction of 10 tanks and 24 vehicles — including the resumption of heavy airstrikes on Dilling, South Kordofan, after months of SAF air restraint linked to prior RSF air-defence successes. Simultaneously, RSF drone strikes continue to kill civilians in SAF-held cities: a drone destroyed a residential house in El-Obeid killing 13 including 8 children, part of a documented pattern of near-daily strikes that killed 700+ civilians in Q1 2026. Canada announced new humanitarian assistance as UN Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher's 'world has failed Sudan' warning from April 22 drew global attention; only 16% of the $3 billion 2026 UN appeal is funded. As the war enters Day 1105, 34 million people — 65% of Sudan's population — require urgent humanitarian support with no ceasefire in prospect.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center, AP, Canada Global Affairs
